course_description:
You will learn about the principles and practice of issues management, crisis communication, and reputation recovery. You will develop an understanding of how to identify and manage emerging issues that can threaten an organisation's reputation and prepare for and manage crises.

In this course, students will explore the theory and the practice of stakeholder engagement, and issues and crisis communication.ﾠ Emphasis will be given to scanning of the organisation's environment for issues and crises, assessing and analysing these issues and crises, and stakeholder identification and analysis.ﾠ The approach will demonstrate the linkages to corporate/organisational reputation, and the significant role of effective strategic communication.ﾠ The course is designed to build in students an understanding of the application of theory to practice - of how issues and crises impact on organisations. ﾠ This appication of knowledge will be deepened through working in depth with a client organisation in developing effective strategic communication.

course_aims: The aim of the course is to develop in students a well-founded knowledge and understanding of concepts and practices of stakeholder engagement, issues management, crisis communication, and reputation recovery. Focus is placed on the application of concepts to the practice of issues and stakeholder communication and the appropriate use of Generative AI tools. Students will develop key attributes required by graduates engaged in strategic communication and public relations professional practice.

learning_outcomes.description: Critique issues, crisis and organisational communication approaches (both existing and emerging) that impact an organisations reputation.
Analyse and prioritise stakeholder positions in relation to issues and crisis events.
Design and develop effective strategies for organisational reputation management and recovery.
Begin to acquire cultural competency skills in relation to community consultation.
Apply AI tools ethically and effectively.
Collaborate with colleagues to present issues and crisis management solutions.

assessment_summary.title: Simulated Interview (Part B)
assessment_summary.due_date: 13/04/2026 - 17/04/2026; Interview presentations in Week 7. A detailed schedule for the interviews will be provided.

learning_resources.library_resources: Find the required and recommended resources for this course on the UQ Library website.

learning_activities.topic: WEEK 1: LECTURE PODCAST This podcast lecture will provide an introduction to issues and stakeholder communication, defining the field of relationship management; stakeholder engagement, community consultation, and engagement. An overview of the assessment will be provided and we hear from Stephen Beckett, CEO of Airlines for Australia and New Zealand. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05

WEEK 1: TUTORIAL In this tutorial the tutor will run through the course assessment and podcast lecture structure. Set readings will be discussed. Please make sure you have completed the first set reading prior to coming to the tutorial. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05

WEEK 2: LECTURE PODCAST This podcast lecture will discuss what an expectation gap is and why it is critical to understand community expectations in an issue and stakeholder engagement context. We hear from Susan Scott, Strategic Communication and Stakeholder Relations specialist. Learning outcomes: L04, L06

WEEK 2: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students will conduct activities on identifying client stakeholder groups, stakeholder mapping, and researching stakeholder groups. Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

WEEK 3: LECTURE PODCAST In this lecture podcast we will discuss how to identify internal and external relevant to an issue or crisis. Using a Brisbane City Council example we discuss how to establish flows of information and process that ensures that an organisation is able to preempt issues before they occur and effectively operate. We speak with Liz Spits, Senior Research and Strategy Lead in Brisbane City Council’s Waste and Resource Recovery Division. Learning outcomes: L04, L05, L06

WEEK 3: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students will experience of community consultation tools and methods and debrief on principles for effective engagement. Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Learning outcomes: L06

WEEK 4: LECTURE PODCAST In this lecture podcast students hear from representatives from Brisbane Airport, the client for both assignments. The session will include an introduction to the organisation, it's stakeholder profile, engagement approaches, and early identification of risks, disruptors and issues. Environmental scanning for BAC (identifying, analysing & prioritising existing and potential issues, media coverage, stakeholder matters of concern). A seperate Zoom Q&A with the client will be provided at a later date. Learning outcomes: L06

WEEK 4: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students conduct environmental scanning for BA (identifying, analysing & prioritising existing and potential issues, media coverage, stakeholder matters of concern). Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Learning outcomes: L05

WEEK 5: LECTURE PODCAST In this lecture podcast we will discuss how to craft appropriate key messages and supporting facts and why message consistency is important in a digital era. We hear from Michael Coombes, Media and Communications Director at Elevate. Learning outcomes: L05, L06

WEEK 5: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students will participate in activities to support the development of key messages, and statements in preparation for issue activation interviews. Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Learning outcomes: L05, L06

WEEK 6: LECTURE PODCAST In this lecture podcast we will discuss IAP2 principles and how to apply them in a community consultation context. We speak with Amanda Newberry, IAP2 Trainer, Articulous Communications Founder and Director. Learning outcomes: L04, L05

WEEK 6: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students work through the issue activation with tutor support. Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Any tutorial falls on a public holiday will be rescheduled. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L06
Mid-Semester Break: NO LECTURES AND TUTORIALS

WEEK 7: LECTURE PODCAST How do you work with communities and understand their perspective to prevent issues escalating? In this lecture podcast we talk with Owan Wareham, Executive Director Strategy, Insights and Communications at Queensland Government in the Department of Women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Partnerships and Multiculturalism about working with different communities. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06
WEEK 7: PRACTICAL Students participate in media interviews. Learning outcomes: L02, L05

WEEK 8: LECTURE PODCAST In this lecture podcast we discuss how an issue can escalate into a crisis. We hear from Robert Hoge, author, speaker & strategic communications expert, who lead the Queensland State Health Government response to Covid 19. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06

WEEK 8: TUTORIAL Students consider the role of activists in a crisis or issue situation develop a stronger understanding of early warning signals. Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06

WEEK 9: LECTURE PODCAST This podcast lecture will consider stakeholder communication and its importance to the technology industry in response to the under 16 social media ban. In this episode we speak with Joanna Stevens, Regional Communications Director at Meta. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06

WEEK 9: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students will conduct exercises in working with stakeholders with different socio economic backgrounds. Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06

WEEK 10: LECTURE PODCAST What do you do when the proverbial hits the fan? In this lecture podcast we speak Deputy Police Commissioner Chris Stream on the operations of Queensland Disaster Task Force and the operations of the Queensland Crisis Response Centre. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06

WEEK 10: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students will explore crisis management and image restoration strategies. Please make sure you have completed the reading and completed the lecture podcast prior to coming to the tutorial. Any tutorial falls on a public holiday will be rescheduled. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06

WEEK 11: LECTURE PODCAST In this lecture podcast we discuss what an organisation can do to prepare for a crisis. We will hear from representatives from Brisbane Airport. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06
WEEK 11: TUTORIAL In this tutorial students will work with their tutor to prepare for the week 12 presentations. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06

WEEK 12: LECTURE PODCAST What systems work and how do you sustain a team in a crisis situation? In this lecture podcast we discuss with Liz Taylor, Communications & Engagement Lead at Transurban how to respond to a crisis and share tips and advice in preparation for the crisis activation presentations. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06
WEEK 12: PRACTICAL Students participate in presentations to BA. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L06
WEEK 13: NO CLASSES IN THIS WEEK

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . Course guidelines Communication Expectations While you are a student at UQ, all communication must be conducted according to the UQ Student Code of Conduct . The UQ Library has a helpful Communicate and collaborate online module . Email is the primary way for you to send messages to, and receive information from, the School and our staff. You must use your UQ email address (not a private address) to communicate with staff. You should add a clear subject line, including course code, and a 2-3 word statement. You can send email at any time, however please do not expect responses outside normal working hours (Monday to Friday from ~8am to ~5pm). Emails that constitute bullying, harassment or discrimination against staff contravene the Student Code of Conduct. Emails like this will be reported to the University, and the matter will be pursued as misconduct. Learning activities
